_agent_spawning_comment: Default false. For tests, enable only tiny batches of 5-10 agents, never the full employee roster. Keep worker calls under the configured safe RPM cap.
_allow_autonomous_root_code_writes_comme: Dangerous self-improvement toggle. When false, the agent cannot directly write into the checked-out project source tree; code must go through code_sandbox + propose_code_change. Set true only if you intentionally want autonomous root-code edits.

ai_provider._comment: AI provider configuration. Fill in keys for the providers you want to use; leave the others as YOUR_X_API_KEY_HERE.
ai_provider._recommendation: For self-prompting agents, frontier models with prompt caching (anthropic claude-opus-4-6, openai gpt-4o, google gemini-2.5-flash) are dramatically cheaper than tiered cheap-worker + orchestrator setups, because the orchestrator's variable prompt can never cache. See README ░░ BRING YOUR OWN LLM ░░ for the math.
ai_provider._provider_note: `provider` is the default worker. `andrew_provider` and `artemis_provider` override for specific agents. Set them all to the same value for a single-brain setup (recommended for prompt caching).
ai_provider._orchestration_note: Leave orchestration_provider and worker_provider empty for a single-brain setup. Setting them to different providers enables tiered orchestration (worker + planner) but breaks prompt caching — only do this if you have a specific reason.

openai._comment: OpenAI API (GPT-4o, GPT-4.1, o3, o4-mini). Get key at platform.openai.com

anthropic._comment: Anthropic Claude API. Get key at console.anthropic.com
anthropic._pricing_note: Opus 4.6: $5/M input, $25/M output. Cache writes $6.25/M (5min) or $10/M (1h). Cache hits $0.50/M — huge savings when system prompt is reused across heartbeats.
anthropic._caching_note: Self-prompting agents reuse the same ~30K-token identity every cycle. With Anthropic prompt caching, this is billed once then read at ~10% of normal price. May 25 burn test: 95% cache hit rate, $10.60/hr for a fully autonomous agent running 307 tools.

google_gemini._comment: Google Gemini API. OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Get key at aistudio.google.com/apikey
google_gemini._pricing_note: gemini-2.5-flash paid tier: $0.30/M input (text/image/video), $1.00/M (audio), $2.50/M output (incl. thinking). Context cache $0.03/M. Free tier has RPD limits.

nvidia._comment: NVIDIA API Catalog — free tier, ~40 RPM. Models at build.nvidia.com
nvidia._uncap_comment: When true, max_tokens is not sent to the API — model uses full capacity including internal reasoning.
nvidia._fallback_models_comment: In-provider fallbacks to try when the primary 404s. Only the top-level fallback_order list triggers cross-provider escalation.

xai._comment: xAI Grok API — chat + media. Get key at console.x.ai

openrouter._comment: OpenRouter — gateway to ~hundreds of models through one API. Cheapest option for remote.

vision._comment: Routes nav perception through NVIDIA NIM hosted VLM (free tier, ~40 RPM). Hard-disable local SmolVLM via local_vlm.enabled=false to free GPU memory. max_images=1 because Llama-3.2-vision rejects multi-image requests with 400.

video_production._comment: Dynamic video provider selection. Options: 'veo' (Google Veo 3.1) or 'xai' (xAI Grok Imagine Video).

local_vlm._comment: On-device vision model (SmolVLM on Jetson GPU). Set enabled=false in dev mode so every frame routes to ai_provider.vision (NVIDIA NIM) and the GPU stays free.

external_apis._comment: External (non-LLM) service credentials. All optional; leave empty if not used.

x_twitter._comment: X/Twitter Social API v2 for tweet search. NOT the same as the xAI/Grok API. Dashboard: developer.x.com

_heartbeat_comment: Seconds between AI heartbeats. Higher = fewer API calls = lower cost. Min 15, max 3600. Recommended: 60 (frontier cached), 300 (paid API, cost-conscious), 900 (minimal usage).

hardware._comment: Toggle hardware features. Set to false on machines without the hardware. 'auto' = detect at startup.
hardware.speaker: auto

camera_broker._comment: Single-producer broker for CSI cameras. The broker is the only legitimate opener of /dev/video0 and /dev/video1 — see repryntt/hardware/camera_broker.py.
